page: Microsoft Azure FX32-8ms_v2 is an x86_64 architecture server belonging to the FXmsv2 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 4 physical cores and 8 vCPUs, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions. The server is provisioned with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 84.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or GPUs, though it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core capabilities. The server achieves top-tier performance in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests, alongside strong results in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Multi-core performance, including stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core metrics, falls within the average range. Memory bandwidth is measured at 691.25 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 72.43 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. For machine learning workloads, the hardware delivers average prompt processing speeds across various model sizes, but exhibits weak performance in text generation for medium-sized models.

From a resource density perspective, the FX32-8ms_v2 offers high qualitative cost efficiency for memory-bound workloads that do not require massive parallel CPU scaling. The extreme memory allocation relative to the 8 vCPUs makes it highly specialized. However, the lack of local storage and GPUs represents a clear tradeoff, requiring external network-attached storage for data persistence. This profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, large in-memory databases, caching layers, and enterprise workloads requiring substantial RAM capacity per compute thread.
description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 vCPUs and 4 physical cores running at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features an exceptionally high memory capacity of 672.0 GB, translating to 84.0 GB per core. The processor includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports AVX-512 and AMX instructions. While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is premium storage capable. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core and text processing performance, though multi-core metrics and large-model text generation speeds are average to weak. This server is designed for memory-intensive workloads, in-memory databases, and caching systems requiring massive RAM-to-CPU ratios without the need for high parallel core counts.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 vCPUs and 672 GB of RAM, designed for memory-intensive workloads and in-memory databases.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX32-8ms_v2 is a memory-optimized server with 8 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and a 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon CPU, built for memory-intensive workloads.
tagline: A highly specialized memory-intensive server featuring an exceptional RAM-to-CPU ratio and strong single-core performance for demanding in-memory workloads.
bullet_points: Dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 8 vCPUs at 4.2 GHz
Extremely high memory capacity of 672.0 GB (84.0 GB per core)
Top-tier single-core and text processing benchmark performance
Supports AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX)
No local storage or GPU, requiring external storage solutions
Suited for in-memory databases, caching layers, and memory-bound applications
